# pylopdf

> Python-native PDF editing, extraction and rendering powered by Rust.

Canonical documentation: https://yhay81.github.io/pylopdf/
Japanese documentation: https://yhay81.github.io/pylopdf/ja/
Simplified Chinese documentation: https://yhay81.github.io/pylopdf/zh-cn/
Korean documentation: https://yhay81.github.io/pylopdf/ko/
Source: https://github.com/yhay81/pylopdf
PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/pylopdf/

Core pages:
- Getting started: https://yhay81.github.io/pylopdf/getting-started/
- API overview: https://yhay81.github.io/pylopdf/api/
- Ecosystem recipes: https://yhay81.github.io/pylopdf/ecosystem/
- Migrating from pymupdf: https://yhay81.github.io/pylopdf/migration/
- Benchmarks: https://yhay81.github.io/pylopdf/benchmarks/
- Concurrency: https://yhay81.github.io/pylopdf/concurrency/
- Security: https://yhay81.github.io/pylopdf/security/
- Language policy: https://yhay81.github.io/pylopdf/languages/

Key facts:
- MIT licensed.
- Zero runtime dependencies.
- Approximately 3.5–4.5 MB abi3 wheels for Python 3.10 through 3.14, plus
  cp314t wheels for free-threaded Python 3.14.
- 0-based pages except get_toc/set_toc, which are 1-based for pymupdf compatibility.
- Top-left-origin display coordinates across extraction, drawing and rendering.
- PDF only; typesetting, signatures and PDF/A validation use ecosystem integrations.
